concepts.domain: Independent podcast network / audio media collective
concepts.site_goal: Position Static Hum as a tightly curated independent podcast network where listeners can discover shows, binge episode archives with full transcripts, support hosts via the Hum Club membership, and follow live tour dates — all wrapped in a glossy near-black UI with neon magenta-to-lime accents and a persistent global audio player that never breaks playback between pages.
audience: Independent show hosts shopping for a network home
concepts.description: Static Hum is positioned like a record label for talk audio: a roster of 11 shows spanning long-form interview, narrative non-fiction, niche comedy, and a flagship news roundup. The site reads like a late-night radio control room rendered as glossy chrome — near-black backgrounds, brushed-metal panel borders, magenta-to-lime gradient ramps used as accent fills and CTA washes, and a live oscilloscope-style waveform that decorates hero blocks and visually echoes the bottom-anchored persistent audio player. Typography pairs a wide industrial display sans (uppercase, slightly condensed) with a humanist text sans for body copy. Density is medium-high: tile grids of shows, episode lists with transcript snippets, and host headshots over magenta gradient gel-lighting. Every interaction stays inside the shell — clicking 'Play' anywhere docks the episode into the bottom player; navigating between Show pages, the Archive, and the Club tier never interrupts the audio.
concepts.motif: Animated oscilloscope waveform that responds to the currently-playing episode, with glossy chrome panel chrome and magenta→lime gradient washes used as section separators and CTA glow

pages.layout_pattern: Full-bleed hero with live waveform animation behind a rotating 'Now Playing Across The Network' marquee; followed by a 3x3 illuminated tile grid of featured shows, a horizontal-scroll 'New This Week' episode rail, a Hum Club membership pitch band with magenta gradient wash, and a tour-dates ticker strip above the footer
sections: Hero with animated waveform + rotating now-playing strap
Featured shows tile grid (3x3, glossy chrome cards)
New This Week horizontal episode rail with inline play buttons
Hum Club membership pitch band (lime CTA on magenta gradient)
Footer with show index, socials, RSS, advertise

pages.layout_pattern: Filterable roster wall — left rail filters (genre, host, frequency, length) and a dense masonry of show cards. Each card shows cover art, host avatars, last-episode badge, and a hover state that swaps to a 6-second waveform preview
sections: Masonry show card wall with hover waveform preview

pages.layout_pattern: Cinematic show landing — full-width parallax cover art with diagonal magenta-to-lime gradient overlay, sticky show header with subscribe-everywhere chip rack, three-column block (about / hosts / sponsors), then a reverse-chronological episode list with seasonal accordion grouping
sections: Full-bleed show key art with gradient overlay and title lockup
Sticky show header (logo, subscribe chips: Apple, Spotify, RSS, Overcast, Pocket Casts)
About the show + run dates + cadence
Season-grouped episode list with play/queue/transcript actions

pages.layout_pattern: Two-column reader layout — left column holds episode metadata, a full-width interactive waveform with chapter markers and clickable timestamps; right column holds the synced auto-scrolling transcript with speaker color-coding; below the fold a 'Mentioned in this episode' link list, guest bio card, and 'Up next' queue suggestions
sections: Episode header (title, show, hosts, runtime, publish date)
Interactive waveform with chapter markers + timestamp jump
Player controls (speed, skip, sleep timer, queue)
Synced transcript panel with speaker labels and search-within-transcript

pages.layout_pattern: Editorial host roster — full-bleed portrait grid where each tile uses a duotone magenta/lime treatment, hover reveals a signature waveform line and a pull-quote; detail view opens an inline drawer with bio, shows hosted, social links, and a 'best of' episode picker
sections: Search hosts by name or show
Inline expanding host drawer (bio, shows, socials, best-of picks)

pages.layout_pattern: Tour calendar in two views — map view with pulsing magenta pins over a dark vector basemap, and list view grouped by month with venue, city, supporting shows, ticket CTA, and a 'meet & greet for Hum Club members' badge where applicable
sections: Date cards with venue, city, supporting shows, ticket CTA

pages.layout_pattern: Conversion-focused membership landing — hero with looping waveform aurora, three-tier price comparison table (Listener / Insider / Patron) with lime accent on the recommended tier, member perks grid, member-only feed preview blurred behind a gradient gate, and member testimonials with audio-clip play buttons
sections: Membership hero with aurora waveform loop
Perks grid (bonus eps, ad-free feeds, Discord, merch credit, live access)

pages.layout_pattern: B2B pitch deck as a scrollable single-page — audience demographics with animated counters, downloadable rate card, host-read vs programmatic comparison, case-study carousel with brand logos, and an inquiry form with budget range slider
sections: Case study carousel with brand logos
Partnership inquiry form with budget slider

message_intent: Signal curatorial taste and editorial point of view
Make every show one tap from playback
Convert casual listeners into paying Hum Club members
Pitch the network as a credible ad / sponsorship buy
Drive live ticket sales without leaving the audio session
content_model: Show (cover art, host refs, sponsor refs, cadence, RSS, subscribe links)
Episode (audio file, chapters, transcript, guest refs, mentioned links)
Host / Voice (portrait, bio, shows hosted, socials)
Tour date (venue, city, datetime, supporting shows, ticket URL, member-access flag)
interactions: Global persistent audio player docked at bottom with waveform scrubber, speed, queue, and sleep timer
Click-to-play any episode tile without losing your place on the page
Auto-scrolling transcript that syncs to playback position
Map ↔ list toggle on tour dates
Filter shows by genre / cadence / length
Hover-preview a 6-second waveform on show cards
Subscribe-everywhere chip rack (Apple, Spotify, RSS, Overcast, Pocket Casts)
concepts.mobile_behavior: Single-column reflow with a persistent mini-player pinned above the bottom tab bar; transcripts collapse into a tap-to-expand panel; tour-date map collapses to list-first with optional map drawer.

concepts.domain: Independent podcast network / late-night talk collective
concepts.site_goal: Make Frequency Room feel like a high-design listening lounge for night owls — a glossy magenta-lit broadcast booth where each show feels like its own intimate channel, the episode archive reads like a deep crate of liner-noted records, and the Inner Circle membership tier feels like getting handed an after-hours key.
audience: Night-owl listeners who consume long-form audio
Brand sponsors seeking premium host-read placements
Live taping ticket buyers and superfans
concepts.description: Frequency Room leans editorial and atmospheric where Static Hum leans aggressive. The aesthetic is a late-night FM control room — near-black canvas, hairline lime dividers, magenta neon used sparingly as glow accents around hero portraits and CTA buttons, and a recurring concentric-ring waveform motif (think a vinyl groove crossed with a tuner dial) that animates subtly as audio plays. Typography pairs a tall geometric grotesk for display with a slab-leaning text serif for episode body copy and transcripts, giving the archive a 'liner notes' feeling. Density is medium-high: long scrolling pages with rich metadata, color-coded show channels (each show gets its own accent hue derived from the magenta-lime gradient range), and a global player that minimizes into a vertical waveform rail along the right edge on desktop. Imagery favors moody studio photography of hosts in low magenta light.
concepts.motif: Concentric tuner-dial waveform rings that pulse with playback, paired with hairline lime dividers and soft magenta neon halos around portraits and primary CTAs

pages.layout_pattern: Editorial homepage — full-bleed hero with a single 'Tonight on Frequency Room' featured episode (large host portrait under magenta neon, concentric waveform behind), then a horizontal channel selector strip (one chip per show, each with its own accent hue), an editor's-picks two-column 'Crate' block, a member tier teaser card, and a tour dates strip
sections: Tonight on Frequency Room hero (featured episode, neon-lit portrait)
Channel selector strip (one chip per show, accent-hued)
Upcoming live broadcasts / tour dates strip
Network masthead with founding date and manifesto link

pages.layout_pattern: Roster as a 'channel guide' — vertical list of shows styled like an over-the-air TV grid; each row spans the page with channel number, show logotype, hosts, cadence, current-season episode count, latest-episode play button, and a small animated waveform ribbon
sections: Filter by genre, cadence, season state (active / archived / hiatus)
Sort by recently updated, alphabetical, channel number

pages.layout_pattern: Per-show channel page — sticky left rail with show identity (logo, channel number, hosts, subscribe chips, accent color stripe), main column holds a featured-season hero, season selector tabs, and an episode list rendered as 'liner notes' rows with episode number, title, runtime, guest, one-line tease, and inline play/transcript controls
sections: Sticky show identity rail with subscribe chips
Featured-season hero with art and synopsis
Liner-notes episode list (episode #, title, guests, runtime, tease)

pages.layout_pattern: Cross-network episode archive — top bar with full-text transcript search, faceted filters (show, host, guest, year, topic tag, length bucket), and results rendered as dense scannable rows with highlighted transcript snippets where the search term appears
sections: Full-text transcript search bar with autosuggest
Faceted filter rail (show, host, guest, year, topic, length)
Search-result rows with highlighted transcript snippets
Saved searches for Inner Circle members

pages.layout_pattern: Liner-notes episode page — masthead with episode artwork (concentric waveform rings centered), three-column metadata block (hosts / guests / chapters), full transcript rendered as long-form readable prose with speaker drop-caps, sidebar with 'Mentioned' links and 'If you liked this' recommendations
sections: Inline player with waveform scrubber and chapter jump
Long-form transcript with speaker drop-caps and timestamp gutter
If you liked this — recommendation list

pages.layout_pattern: Host directory styled like a 'staff roll' — large portraits in low magenta light arranged in a 4-column grid with handwritten-style signature beneath each name; clicking a portrait expands a full-bleed feature with bio, career timeline, shows hosted, and a curated 'self-selected episodes' list
sections: Staff-roll portrait grid (4-column, low magenta light)
Expanded feature view with bio + career timeline

pages.layout_pattern: Live tour and broadcast calendar — month-grid calendar with neon-pin event dots, sidebar list of upcoming dates, each event card includes venue, city, doors/start time, supporting shows, ticket CTA, and a 'simulcast on Inner Circle' badge where the live tape will stream to members
sections: Month-grid calendar with neon-pin event dots
Event detail cards (venue, time, supporting shows, tickets)
Photo gallery from past live tapings

pages.layout_pattern: Membership lounge — atmospheric hero with looping concentric ring animation, two-tier pricing comparison (Late Pass / Skeleton Key) with lime accent on the recommended tier, perks lockup grid, gated 'after-hours feed' preview with a blurred episode list, member-only Discord ribbon, and an FAQ accordion
sections: Atmospheric membership hero with concentric ring loop
Two-tier pricing comparison (Late Pass / Skeleton Key)
Perks lockup grid (ad-free, after-hours feed, simulcasts, merch, Discord)
Gated after-hours feed preview (blurred episode list)

pages.layout_pattern: Advertiser pitch page — scrollable narrative with animated audience counters, listener psychographic chart, host-read sample audio clips with play buttons, downloadable media kit PDF, partner logos wall, and a tiered inquiry form (sponsorship / brand partnership / live event underwriting)
sections: Host-read sample clips with play buttons
Tiered inquiry form (sponsorship / brand partnership / live underwriting)

pages.layout_pattern: Network manifesto and team — long-form editorial scroll opening with a typewritten-style mission statement, founding timeline rendered as a vertical waveform with year markers, staff portrait grid (operations, producers, sound engineers), and a careers / pitch-a-show sub-section
sections: Operations and production team portrait grid

message_intent: Convey curatorial prestige and editorial atmosphere
Make the archive feel like a deep, searchable crate
Tier the audience: casual ↔ Late Pass ↔ Skeleton Key
Position the network as a premium ad environment
Recruit new hosts via a credible pitch path
content_model: Channel / Show (channel number, accent color, hosts, cadence, seasons)
Season (number, run dates, episode count, art)
Episode (audio, chapters, transcript, guests, mentioned links, mailbag)
Voice / Host (portrait, bio, timeline, shows, self-selected episodes)
Live broadcast (venue, datetime, supporting shows, simulcast flag)
interactions: Global persistent audio player with concentric-ring waveform and chapter jump
Vertical mini-player rail on desktop / docked bar on mobile
Full-text transcript search across the network with snippet highlighting
Channel-hop strip on the homepage to instantly preview each show
Season tabs and self-selected host episode lists
Gated member feed preview with blur
Calendar pin hover for live broadcast detail
asset_needs: Per-show logotypes and accent color tokens
Partner logos and host-read sample clips
concepts.mobile_behavior: Vertical stack reflow; the desktop right-edge waveform rail collapses into a bottom-docked mini-player; archive filters become a slide-up sheet; calendar view defaults to list with a tap-to-open month grid.
